featuretablefilter 0.1.0
  • Get started
  • Reference
  • Articles
    • featuretablefilter Tutorial: Complete Guide to Microbiome Feature Table Filtering

    Filter features based on network connectivity

    filter_by_network_connectivity.Rd

    Removes features that have no significant connections to other features in the network. These disconnected features are likely spurious artifacts, cross-talk, or contamination rather than true biological signals.

    filter_by_network_connectivity(
  table,
  similarity_type = c("mi", "cor"),
  threshold = NULL,
  min_degree = 1,
  min_prevalence = 0.05,
  k = 3,
  verbose = TRUE
)

    Arguments

    table

    A feature table (data.frame or matrix) with features as rows and samples as columns. First column should be feature IDs.

    similarity_type

    Type of similarity to compute: "mi" for mutual information, "cor" for Pearson correlation. Default is "mi".

    threshold

    Similarity threshold for defining edges. If NULL, automatic thresholding is used. Default is NULL.

    min_degree

    Minimum degree (number of connections) required to keep a feature. Default is 1 (features must have at least one connection).

    min_prevalence

    Minimum proportion of samples where a feature must be present for MI calculation. Passed to compute_mutual_information. Default is 0.05.

    k

    Number of nearest neighbors for MI estimation. Passed to compute_mutual_information. Default is 3.

    verbose

    Logical. Print progress and summary. Default is TRUE.

    Value

    A filtered feature table with disconnected features removed. Attributes include:

    n_filtered_out

    Number of features removed

    network_summary

    List with network statistics

    disconnected_features

    Character vector of removed feature names

    Details

    This approach is based on the principle that true biological features typically exhibit coordinated behavior with other features due to ecological interactions, shared environmental responses, or phylogenetic relationships.

    Examples

    # Filter out features with no network connections
# cleaned_table <- filter_by_network_connectivity(my_table)

# Use correlation instead of MI (faster but less sensitive to non-linear patterns)
# cleaned_table <- filter_by_network_connectivity(my_table, similarity_type = "cor")

# Require at least 2 connections to keep a feature
# cleaned_table <- filter_by_network_connectivity(my_table, min_degree = 2)

    Contents
